author: Tania
description: This Dutch oven pot roast is the ultimate comfort food. It's incredibly flavorful, with fall-apart tender beef slow-braised alongside baby carrots and potatoes for a complete one-pot meal.
ingredients: 1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt, plus more to taste
2 large yellow onions, peeled and sliced
2-3 cups beef broth, plus more as needed
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°F.
Season the beef generously on all sides with kosher salt and black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the beef on each side until deeply golden, about 5-6 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onions and sauté until starting to brown, scraping the bottom of the pot to release browned bits. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add red wine (or beef broth) and deglaze the pot. Stir in tomato paste, 2 cups beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, rosemary, and bay leaves. Bring to a low boil.
Return the beef and its juices to the pot. Liquid should cover about half the meat; add more broth if needed. Cover and bake for 1 1/2 hours.
Remove from oven and add carrots and potatoes. Adjust seasoning if needed. Cover and return to oven for 2 more hours, or until meat is fork-tender and vegetables are soft.
Optional: Cook an additional 15-30 minutes for extra tenderness. Garnish with parsley before serving.
notes: Meat: A 3-4 pound beef chuck roast works best. Trim excess exterior fat if desired.
Searing: Do not rush browning; the fond adds significant flavor to the sauce.
Wine: Cabernet Sauvignon or Pinot Noir are recommended. Beef broth may be substituted.
Storage: Refrigerate leftovers in a sealed container.
Freezing: Freeze in individual containers for up to 2 months. Thaw overnight and reheat gently.
personalNotes: The rosemary was overpowering. We added more broth, salt, pepper, and a whole onion. Using Central Markets fire roasted hatch chili tortilla chips made it a lot better. It takes it from 'okay' to 'good'.
title: Dutch Oven Pot Roast

author: Brianna May
description: This stovetop Dutch Oven Chili is hearty, rich, and full of flavor. The bacon, chipotle peppers, and adobo sauce add a delicious smokiness. This recipe works well in either an enameled or cast iron Dutch oven.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon granulated garlic or garlic powder
1/2 teaspoon salt, plus more to taste
1 (28 ounce) can crushed tomatoes, plus about 1/2 cup water
1 (7 ounce) can chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, use only 1-3 chopped peppers & all remaining sauce
instructions: In a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat, fry the bacon until browned.
Add the ground beef and break it into crumbles with a spoon. Reduce heat to medium.
Add all of the spices and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the bell pepper and onion. Sauté for 4-5 minutes.

Add the crushed tomatoes, diced tomatoes, chipotles, adobo sauce, Worcestershire, kidney beans, and pinto beans. Add about 1/2 cup water to the empty crushed tomato can, swirl, and add to the pot. Bring to a light boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer covered for at least 60 minutes, stirring occasionally and adjusting heat as needed.
Remove the lid. If needed, simmer uncovered until the chili reaches desired thickness.
notes: Ground Beef: 85/15 is recommended for best flavor. If using leaner ground beef, ground turkey, or chicken, add a little olive oil to make up for lost fat.
Smoked Paprika: Use smoked (not regular).
Chipotles: Use only 1-3 peppers depending on desired heat. For less spice, use just the adobo sauce. Leftover peppers can be stored in the fridge.
Honey: Substitute for sugar.
Beans: Substitute for your favorite beans.

Simmer: Cook at least 60 minutes so flavors develop. Optionally cook in the oven at 250F for up to 2-3 hours (stir/check to ensure it is not burning), or use a slow cooker (low 6-8 hours, high 3-4 hours).
Freeze leftovers: Store in the fridge for 3 days or in the freezer for up to 3 months. Portion into freezer bags or containers and reheat directly from frozen on the stove.
personalNotes: The chili's can make it pretty hot. I only put in two and it made my mom sick (she is not good with spices). I thought two was fine. It was easy to cook, good with shredded cheese and crackers, and it was really easy to freeze and recook.
title: Dutch Oven Chili

author: Jamie Vespa MS, RD
description: This restaurant-worthy Creamy Lemon Chicken Pasta is easy enough for weeknights, yet impressive enough for company. It's a family-favorite recipe and a guaranteed crowd pleaser.
equipment: Large sauté pan or high-sided skillet
ingredients: 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasts, thinly sliced into cutlets
1 lb pasta of choice (bucatini, spaghetti, pappardelle, or fettuccine)
1/3 cup finely chopped fresh parsley, plus more for garnish
1/3 cup grated Parmigiano Reggiano cheese, plus more for garnish
1 tablespoon lemon zest plus 3 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
instructions: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a boil.

Prepare the chicken: Place chicken in a bowl with olive oil, lemon zest, and spices. Toss to coat. Heat a large sauté pan over medium heat and cook chicken until golden and internal temperature reaches 165°F, about 7-10 minutes. Transfer to a plate and keep warm.
Cook pasta until just shy of al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water before draining.
Prepare the sauce: Add butter to the same pan along with onion. Cook over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add heavy cream, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a simmer and cook uncovered until slightly thickened, about 5-6 minutes. Stir in parsley, Parmesan, lemon zest, and lemon juice.

Add pasta to the sauce along with 1/2 cup reserved pasta water. Increase heat to medium-high and toss vigorously until pasta is coated in a glossy sauce, about 2 minutes. Add more pasta water if needed.
Slice chicken and place on top of pasta. Garnish with extra parsley, Parmesan, cracked black pepper, and a squeeze of lemon juice.
notes: Optional add-ins: fresh baby spinach or arugula, sun-dried tomatoes, roasted broccoli, or sautéed mushrooms. Add vegetables to the sauce after stirring in parsley, Parmesan, and lemon.
Storage: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheating: Gently rewarm in a skillet over medium heat with a splash of broth to loosen the sauce. Finish with fresh lemon juice.
title: Creamy Lemon Chicken Pasta

author: Sally
description: Make a sweet Dutch baby pancake using this simple, 8-ingredient recipe. It bakes up puffed and golden with crisp edges and a soft center, and can easily be adapted for sweet or savory toppings.
equipment: 10-inch oven-safe skillet or cast iron skillet
ingredients: 3/4 cup whole milk, at room temperature (or nondairy)
Optional toppings: berries, whipped cream, maple syrup, and/or strawberry sauce
instructions: Place the eggs in a blender and blend on medium-high speed until frothy and fluffy, about 45 seconds.
Add flour, milk, lemon zest, sugar, salt, and vanilla. Blend until fully combined; the batter will be thin.
Set the batter aside to rest for 15 minutes at room temperature, or cover and refrigerate for up to 24 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400°F (204°C). Place butter in a 10-inch oven-safe skillet and place in the oven for 5 minutes to melt. Remove and brush the butter up the sides of the pan.
Pour the batter into the center of the hot pan without swirling.
Bake for 15 minutes, then reduce oven temperature to 350°F (177°C) and bake for an additional 10 minutes, until puffed and deeply golden.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes. Top as desired, slice, and serve.
notes: Milk: Whole milk is preferred, but any dairy or nondairy milk works.
Lemon Zest: Adds subtle freshness; the pancake does not taste strongly of lemon. Can be omitted or replaced with 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon.
Pan Options: If you don't have a cast iron skillet, use any oven-safe skillet around 10 inches, a 10-inch pie dish, a 9-inch square pan, or a 3-quart baking dish.
Storage: Best enjoyed immediately, but leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 5 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
Savory Version: Reduce sugar to 1 tablespoon and increase salt to 1/4 teaspoon. Add chives, parsley, sour cream, garlic powder, and black pepper. Top with smoked salmon, arugula, or pickled onions.
personalNotes: These are good and easy to make. I like to add lemon juice on top of the powdered sugar and fold the dutch baby over like they do it at Ol' South Pancake House. I would add enough powdered sugar and warmed up fresh squeezed lemon juice onto the Dutch baby, but just enough to make the sugar/juice mix a powdery liquid mix. Doing that with cut up strawberries, blackberries, and blueberries is really good.
title: Dutch Baby Pancake

author: Natasha of NatashasKitchen.com
description: This pan-seared steak with garlic butter delivers steakhouse-quality flavor at home. The steaks are beautifully seared on the outside, tender on the inside, and finished with an aromatic garlic butter baste.
ingredients: 1 lb New York strip steaks (2 steaks), ribeye, or top sirloin steaks (about 1 1/4-inch thick)
1/4 tablespoon vegetable oil or other high-heat cooking oil
instructions: Thoroughly pat steaks dry with paper towels. Just before cooking, season generously with salt and black pepper.
Heat a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat until very hot. Add oil and swirl to coat the pan.
Add steaks to the skillet and sear on the first side for about 4 minutes until a deep brown crust forms. Flip and cook another 3-4 minutes.
Using tongs, turn steaks onto their sides to render and sear the fat edges, about 1 minute per edge.

Reduce heat to medium. Add butter, garlic, and rosemary to the pan. Tilt the pan and spoon the melted butter over the steaks continuously for about 1 minute, until the steaks are 5-10°F below desired doneness.
Transfer steaks to a cutting board, loosely cover, and rest for 10 minutes before slicing. Spoon extra garlic butter over the steaks to serve.
notes: Doneness Guide: Medium-rare remove at 125°F (final 130°F); Medium remove at 135°F (final 140°F); Medium-well remove at 145°F (final 150°F); Well-done remove at 155°F (final 160°F).
The internal temperature will continue to rise while the steaks rest.
Serve sliced against the grain for maximum tenderness.
title: Pan-Seared Steak with Garlic Butter

author: Alison
description: This Dutch Oven Chicken Pot Pie is an easy, comforting dinner that feeds a crowd. Using puff pastry, rotisserie chicken, and frozen vegetables makes this a delicious home-cooked meal with minimal prep.
ingredients: 2 cans condensed cream of chicken soup (no liquid added)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Heat a Dutch oven over medium heat and add olive oil.
Remove puff pastry from the freezer and allow it to thaw to room temperature.
Add onion and garlic to the pot and cook, stirring frequently, for about 5 minutes until softened.
Whisk together milk and flour, then add to the pot.
Add all remaining ingredients except the chicken and puff pastry.
Simmer until potatoes are just cooked through. The mixture will be thick; add additional milk or water if a thinner consistency is desired.
Add shredded chicken and stir to combine.
Remove Dutch oven from heat.
Trim puff pastry to fit inside the Dutch oven using the lid as a guide, then place it on top of the filling.
Bake uncovered for 35-40 minutes until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is bubbling around the edges.
Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
notes: If short on time, omit the potatoes and proceed directly to baking after heating the other ingredients.
Uncooked diced chicken breast can be used instead of rotisserie chicken; cook it fully with the onions and garlic before adding other ingredients.
Always bake with the Dutch oven lid off.
personalNotes: I needed to use two of the pastry dough sheets. It was good, set off the fire alarm for my apartment so open up a door or window when cooking this. Clean the dutch oven after cooking cause the residue is kinda hard to remove once it dries.
title: Dutch Oven Chicken Pot Pie

author: Lauren Allen
description: Tender, fall-off-the-bone braised short ribs cooked low and slow in a rich, savory sauce-all in one pot. This comforting dish is perfect served over creamy mashed potatoes for an impressive yet approachable meal.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper, to taste
1 cup dry red wine (such as Côtes du Rhône or Pinot Noir)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add onion and carrots and cook for a few minutes, stirring constantly, until slightly softened. Remove vegetables to a plate.
Season short ribs generously with salt and pepper, then coat on all sides with flour.
Add remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil to the pot and increase heat to high. Add ribs and brown on all sides, about 45 seconds per side. Remove ribs to a plate.
Add red wine to the pot to deglaze, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Bring to a boil and cook for 2 minutes.
Add beef broth, additional salt and pepper if needed, and stir in tomato paste. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Return ribs and vegetables to the pot along with whole sprigs of thyme and rosemary.
Cover with lid and place in the oven. Cook at 350°F for 2 hours, then reduce heat to 325°F and cook an additional 30-45 minutes, until ribs are very tender.
Remove from oven and let rest, covered, for 20 minutes. Skim excess fat from the surface before serving.
Serve short ribs over mashed potatoes with spoonfuls of the sauce.
notes: Slow Cooker: Complete stovetop steps, then transfer everything to a slow cooker and cook on low for 6-8 hours.
Instant Pot: Use sauté mode to brown vegetables and ribs, deglaze with wine, add remaining ingredients, then cook on high pressure for 45 minutes with a 15-minute natural release.
Make Ahead: This dish benefits from resting and can be prepared several hours in advance.
Freezing (Before Cooking): Freeze up to step before oven cooking for 4-5 months.
Freezing (After Cooking): Cool completely and freeze for 3-4 months.
title: Braised Short Ribs

author: Lauren Allen
description: This old-fashioned Peach Cobbler is incredibly easy to make from scratch and can be prepared with fresh or canned peaches, making it a perfect dessert any time of year.
ingredients: 5 peaches, peeled, cored, and sliced (about 4 cups)
instructions: Add sliced peaches, sugar, and salt to a saucepan and stir to combine.
Cook over medium heat for a few minutes until the sugar dissolves and juices release from the peaches. Remove from heat and set aside.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Slice butter into pieces and add to a 9x13-inch baking dish. Place the dish in the oven while it preheats to melt the butter, then remove from oven.
In a large bowl, mix flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Stir in milk just until combined.
Pour batter evenly over the melted butter in the baking dish.
Spoon peaches and their juices evenly over the batter. Sprinkle generously with ground cinnamon.
Bake for 38-40 minutes, until golden brown. Serve warm, optionally with ice cream.
notes: Canned Peaches: Substitute with 1 quart jar of canned peaches, undrained. Skip cooking steps and start at batter preparation.
Gluten-Free Option: Use a gluten-free flour blend such as Cup4Cup or King Arthur Measure for Measure.
Best served warm with vanilla ice cream.
title: Peach Cobbler

author: Joanna Cismaru
description: This Instant Pot Mongolian Beef delivers takeout-style flavor at home in a fraction of the time. The beef is tender, saucy, and perfectly sweet-savory, making it a fast and satisfying dinner.
equipment: Instant Pot or electric pressure cooker
ingredients: 2 pounds flank steak, cut into thin strips
instructions: Toss flank steak strips with cornstarch in a large bowl and let sit for 5 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk together rice vinegar, soy sauce, water, garlic, ginger, brown sugar, and red pepper flakes. Set aside.
Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode.
Add half of the sesame oil to the pot. Once hot, add half of the beef and cook for 2-3 minutes until it begins to brown. Remove and repeat with remaining oil and beef.
Return all beef to the pot and cancel sauté mode.
Pour sauce over the beef and stir to combine.
Close the lid and set Instant Pot to Manual (High Pressure) for 10 minutes.
Quick release the pressure when cooking is complete.
Stir in green onions and garnish with sesame seeds. Serve over rice.
notes: Slicing Tip: Freeze steak for 20 minutes before slicing to make it easier to cut thin strips.
Meat Options: Top round steak, chicken breast, or chicken thighs can be substituted.
No Instant Pot: A stovetop version of Mongolian beef can be used instead.
title: Instant Pot Mongolian Beef

author: Savory Tooth
description: This Instant Pot Broccoli Cheese Soup comes together quickly and easily using a pressure cooker. The result is a rich, creamy soup with tender broccoli and plenty of melted cheddar, perfect for a comforting meal.
equipment: 6-quart Instant Pot or electric pressure cooker
ingredients: Salt and black pepper, to taste
instructions: Select sauté mode on the Instant Pot and melt the butter. Add diced onion, carrots, and celery, and cook for about 5 minutes until onions are soft, stirring frequently. Turn off sauté mode.
Add broccoli florets and 2 cups of water. Stir to combine.
Secure the lid, seal the vent, and pressure cook on high for 5 minutes. Immediately perform a quick pressure release.
Uncover the pot and select sauté mode. Stir in heavy whipping cream and minced garlic.
Gradually add shredded cheddar cheese, stirring constantly until fully melted and well incorporated. Turn off sauté mode.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste and serve hot.
notes: Broccoli: Cut florets into small pieces (no larger than 2 inches) for even cooking. Frozen florets or a mix of broccoli and cauliflower can also be used.
Cheese: Cheddar works best, but other soft shredded cheeses may be substituted.
Liquid: Water can be replaced with chicken or vegetable broth for more flavor.
Texture: For a thicker soup, blend with an immersion blender.
Storage: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator or freezer. Thaw overnight before reheating.
title: Instant Pot Broccoli Cheese Soup

author: Rachel Gurk
description: Instant Pot jasmine rice is fluffy, perfectly cooked, and incredibly easy to make. You don't need a rice cooker to get delicious jasmine rice for stir fries, curries, or any Asian-inspired meal.
equipment: 6-quart Instant Pot or electric pressure cooker
ingredients: 1 1/3 tablespoons oil (coconut oil, olive oil, or butter)
instructions: Place jasmine rice in a fine mesh strainer and rinse thoroughly until the water runs clear.
Add rinsed rice to the Instant Pot insert along with water, oil, and salt. Push down any rice on the sides so it is submerged in liquid.
Secure the lid, set the valve to seal, and pressure cook on high for 5 minutes.
When cooking is complete, allow the pressure to naturally release for 10 minutes.
Quick release any remaining pressure, remove the lid, fluff the rice with a fork, and serve.
notes: Ratio: Keep a 1:1 ratio of rice to water when adjusting quantities. Pressure cook time remains the same.
Yield: This recipe makes about 9 cups of cooked rice.
Diet: Naturally gluten-free.
title: Instant Pot Jasmine Rice

author: John Kanell
description: Chicken Florentine is a comforting Italian-inspired dish featuring pan-seared chicken breasts served in a garlicky spinach cream sauce with parmesan. It's quick, easy, and made with simple everyday ingredients.
instructions: In a bowl, combine the flour, salt, and pepper and whisk together.
Pound the chicken breasts with a mallet until about 1 inch thick.
Season both sides of the chicken with salt and pepper, then dredge in the flour, shaking off any excess.

Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil and 1 tablespoon butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add two chicken breasts, cover, and cook for about 4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (internal temperature of 165°F). Remove to a plate and repeat with remaining chicken using another tablespoon of butter.
Reduce heat to medium. Melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter in the skillet, add garlic, and cook for 30-45 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the white wine and cook, stirring occasionally, until reduced by about half.
Stir in the cream and bring to a simmer. Add the parmesan cheese and stir until melted.
Add the spinach and cook until wilted. Return the chicken and any juices to the pan, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer until the sauce thickens and the chicken is warmed through, about 4 minutes.
Garnish with parsley if desired and serve immediately.
notes: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs can be used instead of chicken breasts.
Swiss chard or kale can be substituted for spinach.
If you don't have white wine, substitute with chicken stock.
Mushrooms can be added and sautéed alongside the garlic.
Serve immediately for best texture, as cream sauces thicken as they cool.
personalNotes: Honestly it wasn't that great. I used a enameled cast iron and I think it was too hot. Also it will be a bit of work to clean the cast iron after cooking if the pan is too hot and food/sauce on the surface burns. I would cook it again but it was just okay. The leftovers were better with rice. I may have liked it with a little more cheese. Also it took me 2 hours to prep and cook this for some reason.
title: Chicken Florentine

author: John Kanell
description: This classic Pecan Pie features a gooey, sweet filling packed with crunchy pecans and baked in a flaky pie crust. It's easy to make and perfect for holidays or any special occasion.
ingredients: 1 cup light or dark corn syrup
4 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
instructions: Position an oven rack in the bottom third of the oven and preheat to 425°F.
Place the unbaked pie crust in a deep-dish pie pan and crimp the edges. If using homemade dough, freeze the crust for 20 minutes. Place the crust on a baking sheet and fill with chopped pecans.

In a mixing bowl, whisk together the corn syrup, sugar, eggs, melted butter, vanilla, and salt until well combined. Pour the mixture over the pecans. Optional: decorate the top with additional pecan halves.

Bake for 15 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 350°F. Continue baking for 35-40 minutes, until the filling is puffed and the center slightly wobbles when shaken. Tent loosely with foil if the crust browns too quickly.
Remove from the oven and cool completely on a wire rack before slicing.
notes: Allow the pie to cool for at least 3 hours before slicing so the filling can fully set.
If the pie is slightly underbaked and the filling is too soft, chill it after cooling to help firm it up.
title: Pecan Pie

author: John Kanell
description: This Creamy Tuscan Chicken is a quick and flavorful weeknight dinner featuring pan-seared chicken breasts in a rich cream sauce with sun-dried tomatoes, spinach, garlic, and parmesan.
ingredients: 4 chicken breasts, pounded to an even thickness
1/2 cup sun-dried tomatoes in oil, chopped
instructions: Pound the chicken breasts to about 1 inch thick, season with salt and pepper, and dredge in flour.

Heat oil and 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add two chicken breasts, cover, and cook about 4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Transfer to a plate and repeat with remaining chicken. Wipe pan if needed.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Add remaining butter to the skillet. Once melted, add garlic and 1 tablespoon flour, stirring constantly for about 45 seconds.
Whisk in chicken broth and oregano. Cook until reduced, then stir in heavy cream, parmesan cheese, and sun-dried tomatoes.
Once the sauce is simmering, add spinach and cook until wilted.
Return chicken to the pan, spoon sauce over the top, warm through, and serve.
notes: Pat chicken dry before dredging so the flour adheres better.
For a gluten-free version, substitute all-purpose flour with a gluten-free flour.
Heavy cream can be replaced with half-and-half for a lighter sauce.
Add red chili flakes for extra heat if desired.
title: Creamy Tuscan Chicken

author: John Kanell
description: These easy homemade Cinnamon Rolls bake up soft, fluffy, and gooey, with tender dough swirled with cinnamon brown sugar and finished with a rich cream cheese glaze.
equipment: Sharp serrated knife or unflavored dental floss
ingredients: 4 2/3 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
6 tablespoons unsalted butter, room temperature (for filling)
instructions: In a large mixing bowl or the bowl of a stand mixer, combine warm milk, granulated sugar, and yeast. Let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
Add remaining dough ingredients and mix on low speed until combined. Increase to medium-low and knead for 8-10 minutes until the dough is springy and tacky but not sticky.
Transfer dough to a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
Grease a 9x13-inch baking pan. Turn dough out onto a floured surface and roll into a 12x24-inch rectangle.
Spread softened butter evenly over the dough, leaving a 1/2-inch border on one long side. Mix brown sugar and cinnamon and sprinkle evenly over the butter.
Roll dough tightly into a log starting from the long side opposite the border. Cut into 12 equal rolls and place cut-side down in the baking dish.
Cover loosely and let rise until almost doubled, about 1 hour. Preheat oven to 350°F.
Bake uncovered for 20-25 minutes until lightly browned. Transfer to a wire rack.
While rolls bake, beat cream cheese, butter, vanilla, and salt until smooth. Add powdered sugar and mix until combined, adding milk as needed for desired consistency.
Spread one-third of the glaze over the rolls immediately after baking. After 5 minutes, spread remaining glaze and serve warm.
notes: Knead the dough thoroughly for best texture, either with a stand mixer or by hand.
Frosting the rolls in two stages allows the glaze to melt into the rolls while still building a thick topping.
Store covered at room temperature for up to 2 days.
title: Cinnamon Rolls

author: John Kanell
description: These moist carrot cake cupcakes are packed with warm spices, fresh grated carrots, and chopped walnuts, then topped with a rich cream cheese frosting. Perfect when you want all the flavors of carrot cake in cupcake form.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a 12-cup muffin pan with paper liners.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, ginger, and nutmeg.
In a medium bowl, whisk together oil, granulated sugar, brown sugar, eggs, and vanilla until well combined.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until almost combined. Fold in grated carrots and walnuts until evenly distributed.
Divide the batter evenly among the cupcake liners, filling each about three-quarters full.
Bake for 17-20 minutes, until the centers spring back when lightly touched or a toothpick inserted comes out clean.
Cool briefly in the pan, then transfer cupcakes to a wire rack to cool completely.
To make the frosting, beat cream cheese and butter together until smooth. Gradually add powdered sugar on low speed, then add vanilla and salt and beat until light and fluffy.
Frost cooled cupcakes using a spatula or piping bag. Store covered in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.
notes: For best results, weigh the flour or spoon and level it to avoid dense cupcakes.
Grate carrots by hand for better moisture and texture; pre-shredded carrots tend to be dry.
Let cupcakes cool completely before frosting to prevent melting.
Vegetable or canola oil works best; melted butter can be substituted but results in a slightly less tender crumb.
title: Carrot Cake Cupcakes

author: John Kanell
description: This Pineapple Upside Down Cake is simple, delicious, and beautifully finished with a caramelized pineapple and cherry topping-so striking that it needs no additional decoration.
equipment: 9-inch pie pan or round cake pan
ingredients: 1 (20 oz) can pineapple slices in juice, drained
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Pour melted butter into a 9-inch round cake pan or pie dish, swirling to coat the bottom and sides. Sprinkle brown sugar evenly over the bottom.
Blot pineapple slices dry. Place one slice in the center of the pan and arrange six around it. If desired, cut additional slices in half and line the sides. Place cherries in the centers and gaps.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate container, whisk together sour cream and milk.
In a large mixing bowl or stand mixer, beat butter until creamy. Add granulated sugar and beat until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes.
Add eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition, then beat in vanilla.
On low speed, add one-third of the flour mixture, then half of the sour cream mixture, alternating until fully combined. Scrape the bowl as needed.
Pour batter evenly over the topping and spread gently to the edges.
Place pan on a baking sheet and bake for 40-45 minutes, until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out with a few moist crumbs. Tent with foil if browning too quickly.
Cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes, then carefully invert onto a serving plate. Cool an additional 20 minutes before serving.
notes: Blot pineapple and cherries thoroughly to prevent a soggy cake.
Weigh flour or spoon and level for accuracy.
All dairy and eggs should be at room temperature for best texture.
For extra pineapple flavor, substitute pineapple juice for the milk.
title: Pineapple Upside Down Cake

author: John Kanell
description: These homemade Coconut Macaroons come together quickly with simple ingredients and minimal effort. They're chewy, lightly crisp on the outside, and optionally dipped or drizzled with chocolate for an extra indulgent treat.
ingredients: 6 ounces semi-sweet chocolate chips or chopped chocolate (optional)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 325°F. Line two rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, stir together the coconut, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla extract, and salt.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites on high speed until stiff peaks form, about 3 minutes.
Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the coconut mixture until just combined.
Using a 1 1/2-tablespoon scoop, portion the batter onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing cookies about 1 inch apart.
Bake one sheet at a time for 20-25 minutes, until the tops and bottom edges are golden brown. Let cool completely on the pan.
If using chocolate, melt it in a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second intervals, stirring between each, until smooth.
Dip the bottoms of the cooled cookies into the melted chocolate or drizzle chocolate over the tops. Let set at room temperature or chill briefly to firm.
notes: Using the entire can of sweetened condensed milk will result in extra gooey macaroons that spread slightly more.
Chocolate can be skipped entirely; the cookies are delicious on their own.
To set chocolate quickly, place the tray in the refrigerator or freezer for 10-20 minutes.
Store cookies in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1 week.
title: Coconut Macaroons

author: John Kanell
description: This easy one-pot Chili Mac has tons of melty cheese, hearty ground beef, and the perfect amount of spice. It's a comforting, family-friendly dinner that's ready in under 40 minutes and great for make-ahead meals.
ingredients: 1 green bell pepper, seeded and diced
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef, onion, bell pepper, and garlic. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the beef is browned and the onion is tender, about 6 minutes.
Stir in the chicken broth, kidney beans, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, chili powder, tomato paste, cumin, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Stir in the pasta and cook, stirring frequently to prevent sticking, until the pasta is tender but still slightly firm, about 12 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat. Sprinkle the shredded cheddar cheese evenly over the top and cover with a lid. Let sit for about 5 minutes, until the cheese is melted and the sauce thickens.
Serve immediately with sour cream and fresh parsley, if desired.
notes: Use freshly grated cheese for the best melting and texture; pre-shredded cheese may not melt as smoothly.
Stir the pasta frequently while cooking to prevent it from sticking to the bottom of the pot.
Cook the pasta just until al dente, as it will continue to soften as it sits.
For variations, substitute black beans for kidney beans, omit the beans, or add vegetables like corn or spinach.
Top with jalapeños, hot sauce, green onions, avocado, or a squeeze of lime for extra flavor.
title: Chili Mac

author: John Kanell
description: This gourmet-style Beef Tenderloin is incredibly tender, juicy, and melt-in-your-mouth delicious. The whole tenderloin is pan-seared, slathered in garlic herb butter, and roasted to perfection for an elegant yet approachable main dish.
equipment: Meat probe thermometer or instant-read thermometer
instructions: Pat the tenderloin very dry with paper towels. Trim away excess fat and silver skin with a sharp knife.
Cut the roast in half crosswise. If the ends are thinner, tuck them under so each piece is evenly thick.
Tie the tenderloins with butcher's twine at 1-inch intervals to form a tight cylinder. Season all over with salt and pepper.
Line a large rimmed baking sheet with foil and fit with a wire rack. Place the tenderloins on the rack and rest at room temperature for 1 hour.
Meanwhile, mix the softened butter, garlic, rosemary, and thyme until well combined.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Pat the tenderloins dry again, add olive oil to the skillet, and sear the meat on all sides, about 2 minutes per side.
Return the seared tenderloins to the wire rack and let cool slightly, about 10 minutes. Spread the garlic herb butter evenly over the meat.
Roast for 20-25 minutes, until the internal temperature reaches 125°F for medium-rare.
Tent with foil and rest for 10 minutes. Remove twine, slice into 1-inch pieces, and serve.
notes: Many butchers sell tenderloins already trimmed and tied, which can save prep time.
Use about 1 teaspoon of salt per pound of tied tenderloin.
An instant-read thermometer is essential for perfect doneness.
Herbs in the butter can be swapped for oregano, tarragon, or roasted garlic.
title: Beef Tenderloin

author: John Kanell
description: These colorful, moist Red Velvet Cupcakes are soft, tender, and lightly chocolatey, topped with a classic cream cheese frosting. They're festive, easy to make, and perfect for parties or special occasions.
equipment: Electric hand mixer or stand mixer
ingredients: 1 to 2 tablespoons red liquid food coloring or 5 to 6 drops gel food coloring
1 1/2 cups unsalted butter, room temperature (for frosting)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line muffin pans with cupcake liners.
In a large bowl or the bowl of a stand mixer, sift together flour, sugar, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Whisk to combine.
Cut the butter into tablespoon-sized pieces and add to the dry ingredients. Mix on low speed until a sandy mixture forms.
In another bowl, whisk together the eggs, buttermilk, vanilla, and food coloring.
Slowly add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients while mixing on low speed until just combined, scraping down the bowl as needed.
Divide the batter evenly among the cupcake liners.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the cupcakes spring back when lightly touched. Cool briefly in the pan, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
To make the frosting, beat the butter on medium speed until smooth and fluffy, about 2 minutes.
Add the cream cheese, vanilla, and salt, and beat until well combined.
With the mixer on low speed, gradually add the confectioners' sugar until smooth and fluffy.
Transfer frosting to a piping bag and pipe onto cooled cupcakes.
notes: Weighing ingredients with a kitchen scale gives the most accurate results, especially for flour.
For a richer chocolate flavor, increase cocoa powder up to 2 tablespoons and add 1 tablespoon of milk.
If buttermilk is unavailable, make a substitute by adding just under 1 tablespoon vinegar or lemon juice to whole milk and letting it sit for a few minutes.
Add more confectioners' sugar for a stiffer frosting consistency.
title: Red Velvet Cupcakes

author: John Kanell
description: Hush Puppies are a Southern classic-crispy on the outside, fluffy on the inside, and perfect for fish fries or family barbecues. These are easy to make with simple pantry ingredients and fry up beautifully golden.
ingredients: Peanut or vegetable oil, for frying
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, baking soda, and black pepper.

In a separate bowl, whisk together the buttermilk and egg until well combined. Add the wet mixture and grated onion to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Let the batter rest for 10 minutes.
Heat about 2 inches of oil in a large Dutch oven or heavy pot to 350°F. Line a baking sheet or plate with paper towels.
Using two spoons, drop tablespoon-sized portions of batter into the hot oil, working in batches to avoid overcrowding.
Fry until golden brown, turning halfway through, about 3-4 minutes total.
Remove hush puppies with a slotted spoon or spider strainer and drain on paper towels. Serve hot.
notes: Do not overmix the batter or the hush puppies will be dense instead of fluffy.
Letting the batter rest allows the cornmeal to absorb moisture and thicken properly.
Use a thermometer to maintain oil temperature at 350°F for best results.
Fry in small batches so the oil temperature does not drop too low.
title: Hush Puppies

author: John Kanell
description: Creamy, fluffy garlic mashed potatoes made with Yukon gold potatoes, butter, milk, and sour cream. This classic side dish is easy to prepare and perfect for holidays, family dinners, or any comforting meal.
ingredients: 2 1/2 pounds Yukon gold potatoes, peeled and cut into large pieces
instructions: Cut the potatoes into large, even pieces (about 1 1/2 inches). Place them in a large pot with the garlic cloves and salt. Cover with cold water by about 1 inch.
Bring the pot to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for about 20 minutes, until the potatoes are fork-tender but not falling apart.
Meanwhile, heat the milk and butter in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the butter is melted and the milk is warm. Remove from heat.
Drain the potatoes and garlic and return them to the pot. Pour the warm milk mixture over the potatoes.
Mash just until smooth using a potato masher or ricer. Do not overmix.
Stir in the sour cream and black pepper. Adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed. Serve warm.
notes: For roasted garlic flavor, roast a whole head of garlic and add the softened cloves during mashing instead of boiling garlic with the potatoes.
Serve topped with extra butter, chopped chives, bacon, or shredded cheese.
Do not overmash, as this can make the potatoes gummy.
An Instant Pot method can also be used following Instant Pot mashed potato instructions.
title: Garlic Mashed Potatoes

author: John Kanell
description: These Air Fryer Sweet Potato Fries are crispy on the outside, tender on the inside, and incredibly easy to make. Perfect as a quick snack or simple side dish, they're lightly seasoned and ready in under 25 minutes.
instructions: Peel the sweet potatoes if desired. Cut them in half lengthwise, then cut into 1/4-inch-thick planks and slice into 1/4-inch-thick sticks.
Place the sweet potato sticks in a large bowl and toss with olive oil, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and paprika until evenly coated.
Preheat the air fryer to 375°F. Add the sweet potatoes to the basket and air fry for 6 minutes, then gently shake the basket to stir.
Increase the temperature to 400°F and continue air frying for an additional 8 minutes, until tender and crispy.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste and serve immediately.
notes: You can peel the sweet potatoes or leave the skin on based on preference.
Cut fries evenly so they cook at the same rate.
Do not cut fries thinner than 1/4 inch or they may burn or break apart.
Shake or stir the fries halfway through cooking to promote even crisping.
Try other spices such as cumin, curry powder, or cajun seasoning for variation.
title: Air Fryer Sweet Potato Fries

author: John Kanell
description: This classic Tres Leches Cake is light, airy, and soaked in a rich three-milk mixture that makes every bite incredibly moist and flavorful. Finished with a fluffy whipped cream topping, it's an easy yet impressive dessert.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Butter or spray a 13x9-inch baking dish.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a large bowl, beat the egg whites on high speed until foamy. Slowly add 1/4 cup sugar and continue beating until stiff peaks form.
In another bowl, beat the egg yolks with the remaining 1 cup sugar until pale and fluffy. Beat in the milk and vanilla.
Add the yolk mixture to the flour mixture and mix just until combined.
Gently fold the egg whites into the batter in two additions, being careful not to deflate them.
Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish and bake for 25-30 minutes, until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Let the cake cool until slightly warm. The cake will shrink as it cools.
In a bowl, whisk together the condensed milk, evaporated milk, and 2/3 cup whole milk. Poke holes all over the cake with a fork and slowly pour the milk mixture over the cake.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
In a large bowl, beat the heavy cream and powdered sugar until soft peaks form. Spread evenly over the chilled cake.
Garnish with fresh berries if desired and serve chilled.
notes: Use a kitchen scale or spoon-and-level method to measure flour accurately.
Separate eggs while cold, but allow whites to come to room temperature before whipping.
Fold egg whites gently to keep the cake light and airy.
The cake can be covered and refrigerated for up to 3 days before serving.
title: Tres Leches Cake

author: John Kanell
description: This classic Prime Rib recipe is rich, tender, and incredibly flavorful, making it perfect for holidays or special occasions. The roast is rubbed with herbs and garlic, roasted at high heat for a beautiful crust, then finished low and slow for a perfectly cooked interior.
equipment: Large cast-iron skillet or roasting pan
ingredients: 1 (8- to 10-pound) bone-in beef prime rib roast
instructions: Using a sharp knife, cut the bones away from the roast by slicing between the rib rack and the meat, keeping the ribs intact. Set the ribs aside.
Loosely cover the roast with plastic wrap and let it stand at room temperature for 3 hours to ensure even cooking.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, rosemary, thyme, salt, pepper, and garlic.
Pat the roast dry with paper towels and rub the herb mixture evenly over all sides.
Place the ribs back onto the underside of the roast and tie securely with butcher's twine at 1-inch intervals.
After about 2½ hours of resting, preheat the oven to 500°F.
Place the roast rib-side down in a large cast-iron skillet or roasting pan. Roast at 500°F for 15 minutes.
Reduce the oven temperature to 325°F and continue roasting for 2 to 2 hours 10 minutes, until the internal temperature reaches 125°F for medium-rare.
Transfer the roast to a cutting board, tent loosely with foil, and let rest for 20-30 minutes.
Remove twine and ribs, slice to desired thickness, and serve.
notes: Letting the roast rest at room temperature before cooking helps it cook evenly.
Always use a thermometer to check doneness without repeatedly opening the oven.
The roast will continue to rise 5-10°F in temperature while resting.
Outer slices will be more cooked than the center, which is normal for prime rib.
title: Prime Rib

author: John Kanell
description: These Instant Pot Sweet Potatoes are an easy, no-fuss side dish that turns out perfectly soft and fluffy every time. They're ideal when you need a quick, hands-off way to cook whole sweet potatoes.
equipment: Instant Pot or electric pressure cooker
ingredients: 2 to 4 sweet potatoes, all similar in size
instructions: Scrub the sweet potatoes thoroughly and prick each one a few times with a fork.
Place the trivet in the Instant Pot and arrange the sweet potatoes in a single layer on top. Add the water.
Secure the lid and make sure the steam valve is set to seal.
Cook on high pressure based on potato size: 20 minutes for small, 30 minutes for medium, or 45 minutes for large potatoes.
Allow the pressure to naturally release for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Carefully remove the sweet potatoes with tongs and serve hot, or let cool to room temperature before storing.
notes: There is no need to peel the sweet potatoes; the skins are edible and will soften during cooking.
Fewer fork pricks will reduce skin splitting, but pricking is necessary to prevent bursting.
If the potatoes are not fully tender after cooking, return them to high pressure for an additional 5 minutes.
Cook times depend on size: small (6-8 oz), medium (9-14 oz), large (15 oz or more).
Store cooked sweet potatoes in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
title: Instant Pot Sweet Potatoes

author: John Kanell
description: These soft and chewy chocolate chip cookie bars are incredibly easy to make and packed with chocolatey goodness, featuring rich brown sugar, vanilla, and a hint of salt. Perfect for slicing and sharing.
ingredients: 3/4 cup unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line an 8x8-inch baking pan fully with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk together the melted butter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar for about 30 seconds until a thick slurry forms.
Add the eggs and vanilla extract and whisk for about 1 minute until fully combined.
Add the flour mixture to the butter mixture and stir with a silicone spatula until almost fully combined.
Reserve about 1/4 cup of the chocolate chips and fold the remaining chips into the dough.
Dollop the dough into the prepared pan and spread into an even layer using an offset spatula, leaving some texture on top. Sprinkle with the reserved chocolate chips.
Bake for about 55 minutes, until the edges are set and golden and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out with a few moist crumbs.
Let cool in the pan for about 1 hour before lifting out with the parchment and slicing into bars.
notes: Sprinkle flaky sea salt over the bars just after baking for a salty-sweet contrast.
Let the bars cool completely before slicing for clean edges.
Store in an airtight container at room temperature for 3 to 5 days.
To keep bars extra soft, add a few marshmallows to the storage container.
Bars can be wrapped and frozen for up to 3 months.
title: Chocolate Chip Cookie Bars

author: John Kanell
description: These Mini Cheesecakes have a crunchy graham cracker pecan crust and a rich, creamy filling that bakes quickly. They're easy to make, perfectly portioned, and ideal for customizing with your favorite toppings.
equipment: Shot glass or small flat measuring cup
ingredients: 1/4 cup sour cream, room temperature (60 g)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a 12-cup muffin pan with paper liners.
Add the graham crackers and toasted pecans to a food processor and pulse until finely crushed. Add the sugar and cinnamon and pulse to combine.
Drizzle 4 tablespoons of the melted butter over the crumbs and pulse until evenly moistened. If the mixture feels dry, add the remaining tablespoon of butter.
Divide the crust mixture evenly among the muffin cups (about 2 packed tablespoons each) and press firmly into an even layer. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, until fragrant. Set aside to cool slightly.
In a stand mixer, beat the cream cheese on medium speed for 2 to 3 minutes until smooth and creamy.
Add the sugar and salt and mix until fully combined, scraping down the bowl as needed.
Add the sour cream and vanilla extract and mix until smooth. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing on medium-low until just incorporated.
Scoop a scant 1/4 cup of filling into each muffin cup, filling nearly to the top.
Bake for 15 to 17 minutes, until the centers jiggle slightly when shaken.
Cool to room temperature in the pan, then remove and refrigerate until chilled before serving.
notes: If the cheesecakes crack, simply cover with toppings such as whipped cream, fruit, or chocolate sauce.
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Freeze cooled cheesecakes for up to 3 months and thaw overnight in the refrigerator.
title: Mini Cheesecakes

author: John Kanell
description: These bright and zingy Lemon Bars have a buttery shortbread crust topped with a smooth, tangy lemon filling. They're easy to make, perfectly balanced between sweet and tart, and ideal for make-ahead desserts.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour, divided (2 1/2 cups for crust, 1/2 cup for filling)
2/3 cup powdered sugar, plus more for dusting
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a 9x13-inch baking dish with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together 2 1/2 cups flour, powdered sugar, and salt. Pour in the melted butter and mix until fully combined.
Press the mixture evenly into the prepared pan to form the crust. Bake for about 20 minutes, until lightly golden.
While the crust bakes, add the granulated sugar and lemon zest to a food processor and pulse until the zest is fully incorporated and the sugar is pale yellow.
Transfer the lemon sugar to a large bowl and whisk in the remaining 1/2 cup flour.
Add the lemon juice and eggs and whisk until completely smooth.
Pour the filling over the warm crust and return to the oven. Bake for about 25 minutes, rotating the pan halfway through, until the filling is set.
Remove from the oven and let cool at room temperature for 1 hour, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Dust with powdered sugar and cut into bars using a clean, sharp knife, wiping the blade between cuts.
notes: Always use fresh lemon juice for the best flavor.
The bars may look slightly underdone when removed from the oven; they will continue to set as they cool.
Lemon bars freeze well. Cut, wrap individually, and freeze for up to 3 months.
Dust with powdered sugar just before serving for best presentation.
title: Lemon Bars

author: John Kanell
description: This classic baked Cheesecake is ultra-creamy with a crunchy graham cracker crust and a smooth, crack-free finish-no water bath required. With simple techniques and a gradual cool-down, it's an easy, reliable showstopper dessert.
equipment: Electric or stand mixer with whisk attachment
ingredients: 1/2 cup sour cream, room temperature (120 g)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a medium bowl, combine graham cracker crumbs, melted butter, and 2 tablespoons sugar until the mixture resembles wet sand.
Press the crumbs into the bottom and slightly up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan. Place the pan on a baking sheet.
Bake the crust for 10 minutes, then remove and let cool while preparing the filling.
Place the cream cheese in a large bowl or stand mixer fitted with the whisk attachment. Beat on medium-low until smooth, about 1 minute.
Add the sugar, salt, vanilla, and lemon juice; beat until fully combined and smooth, scraping down the bowl as needed.
With the mixer on medium-low, add the eggs one at a time, mixing just until incorporated. Mix in the sour cream until smooth.
Pour the filling into the cooled crust and smooth the top.
Bake for 15 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 300°F and bake for another 30-35 minutes, until the edges are slightly puffed and the center gently wobbles.
Turn off the oven and leave the cheesecake inside with the door closed for 1 hour.
Remove and cool at room temperature for 1 hour, then refrigerate for at least 6 hours before serving.
notes: Use the whisk attachment (not the paddle) to ensure a smoother filling without lumps.
Do not increase mixer speed above medium-low to avoid incorporating excess air.
Gently tap the pan on the counter to release air bubbles before baking.
Cool the cheesecake gradually to prevent cracks.
For clean slices, warm a sharp knife in hot water, wipe dry, and clean between cuts.
title: Classic Cheesecake

author: John Kanell
description: These Braised Beef Short Ribs are slow-cooked in a rich red wine and herb sauce until the meat is fall-apart tender. Perfect for cozy dinners, special occasions, or make-ahead meals, they're deeply flavorful and incredibly comforting.
ingredients: 8 bone-in beef short ribs (about 4 pounds / 1.8 kg)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 325°F.
Season the short ribs all over with salt and pepper. Place the flour in a shallow bowl and dredge each rib on all sides, shaking off excess.

Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat until very hot. Add the olive oil, then sear half of the ribs in a single layer until deeply browned on all sides, about 6-8 minutes total. Transfer to a plate and repeat with remaining ribs. Remove excess fat, leaving about 2 tablespoons in the pot.
Reduce heat to medium and add the onion, carrots, celery, and garlic. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring and scraping up browned bits, until vegetables begin to soften.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes until slightly caramelized. Add the red wine and simmer until reduced by about half, roughly 8 minutes.
Add the beef broth and return the short ribs to the pot. Tie the thyme, rosemary, and bay leaf together with twine and add to the pot. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover with a tight-fitting lid and transfer to the oven. Cook for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, until the meat is fork-tender and nearly falling off the bone.
Remove the herb bundle and skim excess fat from the surface. Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the short ribs with the vegetables and sauce, optionally over mashed potatoes.
notes: Cut vegetables into large chunks so they hold their shape and add natural sweetness to the sauce.
If you don't have a Dutch oven, use a large oven-safe pot with a tight-fitting lid.
For a thicker sauce, remove the ribs and simmer the braising liquid on the stovetop for 10-15 minutes.
This dish can be made up to two days ahead and reheated gently; flavor improves over time.
An Instant Pot version can be made by pressure cooking on high for 35 minutes with a 10-minute natural release.
title: Braised Beef Short Ribs

author: John Kanell
description: Sopapilla Cheesecake combines flaky crescent roll layers with a rich, creamy cheesecake filling and a cinnamon-sugar topping. Inspired by the classic New Mexican dessert, this crowd-pleasing treat is best served warm with honey, berries, or whipped cream.
equipment: Electric hand mixer or stand mixer
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F and grease a 9x13-inch baking dish with cooking spray.
Unroll one package of crescent rolls and press it into the bottom of the baking dish, stretching to the edges and pinching seams together if needed.
In a large bowl or stand mixer, beat the cream cheese, 3/4 cup sugar, egg, and vanilla on medium speed until smooth and creamy, about 2 minutes.
Spread the cream cheese mixture evenly over the dough in the baking dish.
Unroll the second package of crescent rolls onto a lightly floured surface. Pinch seams together and roll into a 9x13-inch rectangle. Carefully place it over the cream cheese layer.
Brush the top dough evenly with melted butter.
In a small bowl, mix the remaining 1/2 cup sugar with the cinnamon and sprinkle evenly over the buttered dough.
Bake for about 30 minutes, until the crust is puffed and golden and the center jiggles slightly when shaken.
Let cool for 30 minutes before serving warm. Drizzle with honey if desired.
notes: A metal baking pan works best for even baking and crisp edges.
Pinching crescent roll seams is important to prevent filling from leaking.
Adjust the cinnamon amount to taste for stronger or milder flavor.
Store leftovers covered in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.
title: Sopapilla Cheesecake

author: John Kanell
description: This baked Raspberry Cheesecake is ultra-creamy and swirled with a bright, sweet-tart raspberry sauce. With a buttery graham cracker crust and a smooth, elegant finish, it's a stunning dessert that's perfect for special occasions.
equipment: Stand mixer with paddle attachment or handheld electric mixer
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine the raspberries, 1/3 cup sugar, and lemon juice. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, for 8-10 minutes until bubbling and saucy. Reduce heat to low.

In a small bowl, whisk the cornstarch with 1 tablespoon water until dissolved. Stir into the raspberry sauce, increase heat to medium, and simmer for 1-2 minutes until slightly thickened. Strain immediately through a fine mesh strainer to remove seeds. Set aside to cool.
Preheat the oven to 350°F and spray a 9-inch springform pan with nonstick spray.

In a medium bowl, mix graham cracker crumbs, melted butter, 1/4 cup sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon salt until the texture resembles wet sand. Press evenly into the bottom and 1 1/2 to 2 inches up the sides of the pan. Bake for 10-12 minutes until lightly golden. Cool on a wire rack.
In a large bowl or stand mixer, beat the cream cheese and 1 cup sugar on medium-low speed until smooth, about 2 minutes.
Add the sour cream, salt, and vanilla. Beat until fully combined and smooth, scraping down the bowl as needed.
Add the eggs one at a time, mixing on low speed just until incorporated.
Pour half of the filling into the cooled crust. Drizzle 1/4 cup of raspberry sauce over the batter and swirl gently with a toothpick.
Carefully pour the remaining batter over the top, then drizzle another 1/4 cup of raspberry sauce over the surface and lightly swirl.
Bake for 15 minutes, then reduce oven temperature to 300°F and bake for an additional 30-35 minutes, until the edges are set and the center wobbles slightly.
Turn off the oven and leave the cheesecake inside with the door closed for 1 hour.
Remove and cool at room temperature for 1 hour, then refrigerate for at least 6 hours before serving.
notes: Strain the raspberry sauce while very hot; it thickens as it cools, making it harder to remove seeds later.
Avoid large pools of raspberry sauce when swirling, as this can cause sinking or cracking.
For clean slices, warm a sharp knife in hot water, wipe dry, and clean between cuts.
The cheesecake can be made up to 3 days in advance and stored loosely covered in the refrigerator.
title: Raspberry Cheesecake

author: John Kanell
description: These ultra-easy Peanut Butter Cookies are soft, tender, and melt-in-your-mouth with the perfect balance of salty and sweet. Made with simple pantry ingredients, they bake quickly and set up beautifully as they cool.
ingredients: 1/2 cup unsalted butter, room temperature (113 g)
1/2 cup light brown sugar, lightly packed (100 g)
1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon salt (optional, if peanut butter is unsalted)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Sift the flour and baking powder together, then whisk to combine.
In a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, cream the butter and both sugars until light and fluffy. Add optional salt if needed.
Add the peanut butter and mix until fully incorporated.
Mix in the egg and vanilla extract, scraping down the bowl as needed.
Add the flour mixture and beat just until combined.
Roll the dough into 1-inch balls and place them on the prepared baking sheet.
Flatten each cookie with a fork in a criss-cross pattern.
Bake for about 10 minutes, until the cookies are just set but still soft.
Allow cookies to cool completely on the baking sheet before transferring.
notes: Cookies will look underbaked when removed from the oven; they continue to set as they cool.
Let cookies rest on the baking sheet until fully cooled to avoid breaking.
If your peanut butter is not salty, add 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon salt to the dough.
For a classic look, use a fork dipped in sugar to flatten the cookies.
title: Peanut Butter Cookies

author: Jamie Silva
description: These chewy chocolate chip cookies have ooey-gooey centers, perfectly crisp edges, and melty chunks of dark chocolate. Made easily in one bowl, they can be baked right away or frozen for cookies on demand.
ingredients: 1 cup chopped dark chocolate (or chocolate of choice)
instructions: In a large bowl or mixer, combine melted butter and both sugars. Mix until smooth.
Add the egg and vanilla extract and mix until fully incorporated.
Add flour, baking soda, and salt. Mix just until combined; do not overmix.
Fold in the chopped chocolate.
Using a cookie scoop, form dough into balls (about 1 1/2 tablespoons each).
Refrigerate dough balls for at least 20 minutes, or freeze for later use.
When ready to bake, preheat oven to 350°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place dough balls on the baking sheet and bake for 10-11 minutes, until edges are lightly golden.
Remove from the oven, sprinkle with flaky sea salt if desired, and let cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before serving.
notes: Cookies may appear slightly underbaked when removed; they will continue to set as they cool.
Dough can be frozen for up to 3 months and baked directly from frozen with an extra minute or two added to bake time.
Do not overmix the dough to keep cookies soft and chewy.
title: The Best Chewy Chocolate Chip Cookies

author: Jess Smith
description: These crispy and chewy chocolate chip cookies have golden, crisp edges with soft, chewy centers. Chilling the dough briefly and using properly softened butter are the keys to achieving the perfect texture every time.
equipment: Parchment paper or silicone baking mat
ingredients: 1 cup packed light brown sugar (200 g)
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, beat the butter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar on medium-high speed until light and creamy, about 3 minutes.
Add the eggs and vanilla extract and mix on low speed until fully combined, scraping down the bowl as needed.
Add the flour mixture all at once and mix on low speed just until no dry spots remain. Do not overmix.
Add the chocolate chips and mix on low just until evenly incorporated.
Refrigerate the dough for 20 minutes while preheating the oven to 350°F.
Line a half sheet pan with parchment paper or a silicone baking mat.
Scoop heaping tablespoon-sized portions of dough (about golf-ball sized) and place on the baking sheet with space for spreading.
Bake for 9-12 minutes, until edges are lightly golden and centers are pale and soft.
Immediately tap the baking sheet firmly on the counter a few times to deflate the cookies.
Sprinkle with flaked sea salt if using.
Cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a cooling rack to cool completely.
notes: Measuring flour and sugar by weight yields the most consistent results.
Butter should be soft but still cool to the touch for proper creaming.
Do not skip chilling the dough; it helps create chewy centers.
Cookies will continue to firm up as they cool.
title: Crispy and Chewy Chocolate Chip Cookies

author: Eric King
description: These Best-Ever Chocolate Chip Cookies have crisp edges, gooey centers, and an indulgent amount of chocolate. Using melted butter and a rest period creates deep flavor and the perfect chewy texture.
ingredients: 1 cup dark brown sugar, lightly packed
10.5 ounces dark or semi-sweet chocolate, chopped into chunks
instructions: Gently melt the butter in a saucepan over low heat. Set aside to cool slightly.
In a small bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the brown sugar and granulated sugar. Pour in the melted butter and stir just until combined.
Whisk in the egg and vanilla until smooth and creamy, then whisk vigorously until the mixture lightens in color.
Using a rubber spatula, fold the flour mixture into the wet ingredients until a few streaks of flour remain.
Add the chopped chocolate and continue folding just until evenly dispersed.
Portion the dough into about 18 balls using roughly 2 1/2 tablespoons each or a 1.5-ounce cookie scoop.
Chill the dough balls uncovered in the refrigerator for 2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 350°F and position racks in the top and bottom thirds. Line two half sheet pans with parchment paper.
Place 6 to 7 dough balls per pan and bake for 6 minutes.
Remove the pans and firmly bang them on the oven rack or stovetop to deflate the cookies. Rotate pans and return to the oven.
Bake for an additional 4 minutes, banging the pans once halfway through and once when removed.
Let cookies cool on the pans for 2-3 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack. Sprinkle with flaky salt if desired.
notes: Banging the pan during baking creates ripples and improves texture.
Cookies should look slightly underbaked in the center when removed.
Dough balls can be refrigerated or frozen and baked later.
Weighing ingredients yields the most consistent results.
title: Best-Ever Chocolate Chip Cookies

author: Kiley Heard
description: This Baklava Croissant Bread Pudding is the ultimate mashup of two classic desserts. Buttery croissants are soaked in a rich vanilla bean custard and layered with a gooey honey-nut baklava filling, then baked until golden and served with vanilla ice cream and honey buttermilk syrup.
equipment: 9-inch springform pan or 9-inch square baking pan

groceryIngredients.notes: traditional for baklava is pistachios and walnuts

ingredients: 7-8 large croissants (22 ounces), preferably dried overnight and cut into 1-inch cubes
1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract
2 cups chopped nuts of choice (traditional for baklava is pistachios and walnuts; I did an equal mixture of chopped pistachios, walnuts, and almonds)
2 tablespoons salted butter, melted and slightly cooled
1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract
1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract (for syrup)
instructions: Grease a 9-inch round (preferably a cheesecake springform pan) or 9-inch square baking pan with cooking spray, then line with a sheet of parchment paper, making sure it is tall enough to drape over the edges. Place onto a baking sheet; set aside.

If your croissants weren't dried out overnight, start by lightly toasting them in the oven. Spread the croissant cubes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake at 350F for 7-10 minutes, until LIGHTLY toasted; you want to be careful to not bake the croissant, you just want a light toast on the outside! Remove from the oven and set aside to use later.

In a medium bowl, combine the brown sugar, honey, melted butter, cinnamon, vanilla, salt, and chopped nuts. Mix until well combined. Then, spread a little over half of the mixture into the bottom of the prepared pan in an even layer (reserve the remaining baklava mixture of layering and filling the bread pudding later). Set aside.

Place 1/3 of the croissant cubes into the bottom of the prepared pan, on top of the baklava mixture layer. Press down to compress. Evenly distribute about 1/2 of the remaining baklava mixture all over the top of the croissant cubes. Place another 1/3 of the croissant cubes on top, press down, and evenly distribute the last remaining baklava mixture on top. Finish by topping with the last 1/3 of croissant cubes and pressing down to compress. You should have done 3 layers of the croissant cubes and 2 layers of baklava filling (not counting the layer at the bottom).
Pour the vanilla bean custard all over the bread, making sure all of the pieces are well-soaked. With the back of a wooden spoon, gently press down on the bread to make sure there are no dry parts.
Cover the pan with foil and let rest in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes. *At this point, you can place into the refrigerator covered overnight, or bake right away.*
Preheat the oven to 350°F.

Once ready, remove the foil from the top and bake at 350F for 55-60 minutes, until the top is golden and set, but the middle is still slightly wobbly when shaken. Remove from the oven and let rest for at least 10-15 minutes.

If you are planning on serving the bread pudding with the honey buttermilk syrup, prepare it towards the end of baking. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, add the butter, brown sugar, honey, buttermilk, and salt. Heat the mixture, stirring frequently until it comes to a boil.

Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and allow to simmer for 2-3 minutes. Then, remove from the heat and whisk in the vanilla and baking soda. The mixture will bubble and foam because of the reaction between the buttermilk and baking soda, so make sure your saucepan is tall enough to sustain that!
Whisk for 30 more seconds. At this point, you can either serve it straight from the saucepan or transfer to an airtight jar or container until ready to use.

Once ready, invert the bread pudding onto a serving plate. Slice and serve the bread pudding warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream, a drizzle of the honey buttermilk syrup, and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt.
notes: Stale or lightly toasted croissants absorb the custard best.
The assembled bread pudding can be refrigerated overnight before baking.
Traditional baklava nuts are pistachios and walnuts, but almonds work well too.
The honey buttermilk syrup foams after adding baking soda, so use a tall saucepan.
Serve warm with vanilla ice cream and flaky sea salt for the best experience.
title: Baklava Croissant Bread Pudding

author: Kiley Heard
description: This Brown Butter Blueberry Crumble Cake has the most soft, tender crumb that melts in your mouth with every single bite. The brown butter adds a warm, nutty richness that pairs beautifully with the bright and sweet jammy blueberry layer that runs throughout the cake. Finished with a golden, buttery crumble topping that adds the most incredible crunch to every bite - served warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream for dessert or with a hot cup of coffee for the ultimate cozy breakfast.
ingredients: 1/2 cup (120g) Greek yogurt or sour cream, at room temperature
2 tbsp (30ml) olive oil (or oil of choice)
2 tsp (8g) vanilla bean paste or extract
Juice of 1/2 a lemon, about 1 tbsp (15ml)
3/4 cup (180g) brown sugar, light or dark
1/2 cup (113g) salted butter, at room temperature
2 tbsp (25g) avocado oil or melted coconut oil
Large pinch of salt (optional salted caramel)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350F. Preheat the oven to 350F. Grease a 9” cake or springform pan with butter, then line the bottom with a parchment paper circle. Set aside.

Add the butter to a saucepan over medium-high heat. Allow to melt and brown, while stirring continuously (~5-8 minutes). As the butter continues to cook, you'll notice that it starts to foam up across the surface (after about 2 minutes). Stir frequently to prevent the milk solids from burning. Continue cooking, stirring with a spatula, until the milk solids begin to brown (the brown specks at the bottom of the pan) and the color starts to deepen. Once the brown butter reaches a deep golden amber color and smells nutty, remove from the heat and immediately pour into a large bowl. Set aside to cool slightly (about 15-20 minutes).
Prepare the blueberries. In a medium bowl, mix together the blueberries, lemon juice, flour, and sugar until the blueberries are coated in the flour and sugar. Set aside.

Prepare the crumb topping. In a medium bowl, combine the flour, brown sugar, cinnamon, salt, and butter. Use a fork, your hands, or handheld electric mixer to mix until combined and the mixture is crumbly. Set aside until ready to use.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
To the bowl with the cooled brown butter, add the sugar, Greek yogurt or sour cream, eggs, olive oil (or oil of choice), buttermilk, and vanilla. Whisk until well combined and smooth.

Add the dry ingredient mixture to the wet. Use a wooden spoon or spatula to fold until just combined and there are no flour streaks remaining, being careful not to over-mix (it's okay if the batter is a little lumpy).
Scoop or pour all of the cake batter into the prepared cake pan and spread into an even layer.

Then, disperse the blueberries all over the top of the cake batter, aiming to lay them in an even layer. Finish by sprinkling the crumble all over the top of the blueberries in an even layer (it's totally okay if some of the blueberries are showing).

Bake at 350F for 60-70 minutes, or until the crumble is golden on top, and a toothpick inserted into the middle comes out clean or with moist crumbs (a blueberry streak is okay). Check the cake at 50 minutes, if the topping is browning too quickly, tent the top with foil for the remainder of baking.
Remove from the oven and let cool for at least 20-25 minutes before serving.

While the cake is cooling, prepare the easy salted caramel. In a medium bowl, mix together the maple syrup, almond butter, avocado or coconut oil until well combined and smooth. You can adjust the runniness and saltiness to your liking by adding more maple syrup and salt, if desired (go off of your preferred taste).

Once ready, slice and serve the cake warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream, a drizzle of salted caramel, and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt, if desired. OR, serve it for breakfast with a cup of coffee.
notes: Serve warm with vanilla ice cream for dessert or coffee for breakfast.
Browning the butter adds a nutty depth of flavor.
Tent the cake with foil if the topping browns too quickly.
The salted caramel is optional.
Do not overmix the batter; a few lumps are fine.
title: Brown Butter Blueberry Crumble Cake

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Banana Coffee Cake Pancakes with Warm Vanilla Bourbon Maple Syrup are soft, fluffy, and tender in the middle with a beautifully golden exterior and perfectly crisp edges in every single bite. A warm cinnamon streusel caramelizes on the bottom and melts into the middle as they cook for that classic coffee cake flavor that pairs absolutely perfectly with the ripe banana throughout. Served warm with a pat of butter and a drizzle of warm vanilla bourbon maple syrup - this is the ultimate cozy breakfast you'll want to make every single weekend.
ingredients: 1 cup (240g) brown sugar, light or dark
12 tbsp (170g) salted butter, softened to room temperature
6 tbsp (78g) brown sugar, light or dark
1 cup (265g) mashed banana, about 2 large or 3 medium bananas
2 tsp (8g) vanilla bean paste or extract
1/4 cup (58g) salted butter, melted and slightly cooled
3/4 cup (180ml) maple syrup, amber or dark preferred
1/2 tsp (2g) vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: Start by preparing the cinnamon streusel. In a medium bowl, combine the flour, brown sugar, cinnamon, salt, and butter. Use a fork, your hands, or handheld electric mixer to mix until combined and the mixture is crumbly. Set aside until ready to use.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, brown sugar, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon. Set aside.
In a separate large bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, mashed banana, eggs, and vanilla until well combined.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet, and whisk until just barely combined.

Add the slightly cooled melted butter and whisk until just incorporated. Be careful not to over-mix, you want a few small lumps in the batter! Let the batter rest at room temperature for 10-15 minutes (do NOT skip this - it allows the flour to absorb the liquid, making the pancakes lighter and fluffier).
Heat a large nonstick pan or griddle over medium heat. Grease with butter.

For each pancake, sprinkle about 1-2 tbsp of the cinnamon streusel directly onto the pan. Use a medium cookie scoop or 1/4 cup measuring cup to pour some of the banana pancake batter on top of the streusel. Sprinkle another 1 tbsp of streusel on top of the batter, followed by another small scoop of pancake batter, and finish with a sprinkle of streusel on top (refer to video if needed). I want to emphasize here that there is no ‘perfect' way to do this! You can go about it however you'd like - this is just the way I did it, but feel free to cook them with the streusel in whatever fashion is easiest and the most fun for you! I personally just like to make sure that there is some streusel on both sides of the pancake so it gets nice and caramelized on the outside, and then some streusel on the inside as well.

Cook until little bubbles form on the surface, and then gently flip to cook the other side until golden brown (~2-4 minutes on each side). Once the pancake is flipped, I like to add a little sprinkle of streusel on top of the cooked side so it melts into the pancake as it finishes cooking. You can adjust the heat on your stovetop as needed throughout the cooking process if the pancakes seem like they're browning too quickly.
Transfer the cooked pancakes onto a baking sheet and keep warm in the oven at 200F while you make the rest of the pancakes.

Once you are ready to serve, prepare the warm vanilla bourbon maple syrup. In a in a small pot or pan over low heat for 5 mins, add the maple syrup, bourbon, and vanilla. Stir and heat for just a few minutes, until warm.
Plate and serve the pancakes warm with a pat of butter, a drizzle of the warm vanilla bourbon maple syrup, and a hot cup of coffee.
notes: Do not skip the 10-15 minute batter rest.
Keep finished pancakes warm in a 200°F oven.
There is no perfect way to layer the streusel; do what is easiest and most fun.
The vanilla bourbon maple syrup is optional.
Use some streusel on both sides and inside the pancakes for the best caramelized flavor.
title: Banana Coffee Cake Pancakes (with Warm Vanilla Bourbon Maple Syrup)

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Old-Fashioned Sour Cream Donuts are soft and fluffy with a beautifully delicate interior and a perfectly golden and slightly crisp exterior that makes every single bite absolutely irresistible. The sour cream adds a subtle tang that works perfectly with a pinch of nutmeg to create that signature old-fashioned donut flavor that feels wonderfully nostalgic in the very best way. Fried until perfectly golden and coated in a silky vanilla bean glaze that melts into every crack and crevice - served warm with a hot cup of coffee, these donuts are the classic treat you'll want to make on repeat!
equipment: 3-3.5-inch donut cutter, cup, or mason jar lid
Large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven
ingredients: 1 egg + 2 egg yolks, at room temperature
2 tsp (8g) vanilla bean paste or extract
1 1/2 cups (360g) sour cream, at room temperature
Avocado oil (or neutral oil of choice), for frying
1/2 cup (120ml) milk of choice, preferably at room temperature
2 tsp (8g) vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the cake flour, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg. Set aside.
In a separate large bowl, whisk together both sugars and the melted butter until combined. Add the egg, egg yolks, and vanilla, and whisk until well combined. Then, whisk in the sour cream.
